questions.text: The idea of a 'Supreme God' who could deliver humans from bondage through devotion (bhakti) is found in the:
questions.explanation: The Bhagavad Gita popularized the idea that devotion to a personal god could lead to salvation.

questions.text: The Nayanars were devotees of which deity?
questions.explanation: The Nayanars were a group of 63 saints devoted to Lord Shiva.

questions.text: How many Alvars were there in total?
questions.explanation: There were 12 Alvars, who were saints devoted to Lord Vishnu.

questions.text: Which philosopher advocated the doctrine of 'Advaita' (oneness of the individual soul and the Supreme God)?
questions.explanation: Shankara, born in Kerala, taught that the Brahman is the only ultimate reality.

questions.text: Ramanuja, influenced by the Alvars, propounded the doctrine of:
questions.explanation: Vishishtadvaita (qualified oneness) suggests the soul remains distinct even when united with God.

questions.text: The Virashaiva movement was initiated by Basavanna and his companions in:
questions.explanation: This movement started in Karnataka in the mid-twelfth century.

questions.text: Which of the following was a central belief of the Virashaivas?
questions.explanation: Virashaivas argued strongly for human equality and against Brahmanical ideas about caste.

questions.text: The saints of Maharashtra, like Namdev and Tukaram, worshipped:
questions.explanation: Vitthala is a form of Vishnu, centered around the temple in Pandharpur.

questions.text: Who said, 'He is a Vaishnava who understands the pain of others'?
questions.explanation: This famous line by Narsi Mehta emphasizes the humanitarian aspect of Bhakti.

questions.text: The Nathpanthis, Siddhas, and Yogis criticized ritualism and advocated for:
options: Meditation on the formless Ultimate Reality
questions.explanation: They believed the path to salvation lay in meditation and yoga.

questions.text: Sufis were ________ mystics.
questions.explanation: Sufis were Muslim mystics who rejected outward religiosity and emphasized love for God.

questions.text: The genealogical list of Sufi teachers is called:
questions.explanation: Silsilahs were orders of Sufis, each following a slightly different method of instruction.

questions.text: A Sufi hospice where religious discussions took place was known as:
questions.explanation: Khanqahs or hospices were centers where people from all walks of life met Sufi masters.

questions.text: Who was the founder of the Chishti Silsilah in India?
questions.explanation: He settled in Ajmer and is one of the most revered Sufi saints in the subcontinent.

questions.text: The compilation of Kabir's verses is found in:
questions.explanation: Kabir's ideas were preserved in the Bijak and were also included in the Holy Scriptures of the Sikhs.

questions.text: Kabir's teachings were based on a complete rejection of:
questions.explanation: Kabir ridiculed all forms of external worship of both Brahmanical Hinduism and Islam.

questions.text: Baba Guru Nanak was born in:
questions.explanation: He was born in Talwandi (Nankana Sahib in present-day Pakistan).

questions.text: Guru Nanak appointed ________ as his successor.
questions.explanation: Guru Angad (Lehna) compiled the compositions of Guru Nanak.

questions.text: The script developed by Guru Angad is known as:
questions.explanation: Gurmukhi was a new script used to write the Punjabi language.

questions.text: The holy scripture of the Sikhs is called:
questions.explanation: It contains the compositions of the Gurus and other Bhakti saints like Kabir and Baba Farid.

questions.text: The town of Ramdaspur developed into the city of:
questions.explanation: Amritsar grew around the central Gurdwara known as Harmandar Sahib (Golden Temple).

questions.text: Which Mughal Emperor ordered the execution of Guru Arjan in 1606?
questions.explanation: Jahangir saw the growing Sikh community as a potential threat.

questions.text: The Khalsa Panth was instituted by Guru Gobind Singh in:
questions.explanation: The Khalsa became a political entity representing the Sikh community.

questions.text: Guru Nanak's motto for his followers was:
questions.explanation: It signified right worship, welfare of others, and purity of conduct.

questions.text: Tulsidas wrote 'Ramcharitmanas' in which language?
questions.explanation: Awadhi was the language spoken in eastern Uttar Pradesh.

questions.text: Surdas was an ardent devotee of:
questions.explanation: His compositions are found in Sursagara, Surasaravali, and Sahitya Lahari.

questions.text: Mirabai was a Rajput princess married into the royal family of:
questions.explanation: She became a disciple of Ravidas, an untouchable saint.

questions.text: The 'Shankaradeva' of Assam emphasized devotion to:
questions.explanation: He started the practice of setting up Namghars (houses of recitation and prayer).

questions.text: The Alvars and Nayanars were critical of:
questions.explanation: They preached that love for Shiva or Vishnu was the path to salvation, unlike the paths taught by Jains or Buddhists.

questions.text: The Marathi word for 'holy songs' is:
questions.explanation: Abhangs were the form of Marathi devotional poetry used by the Varkari sect.

questions.text: Who among the following was a woman Nayanar saint?
questions.explanation: She adopted the path of extreme asceticism to attain her goal.

questions.text: Who among the following was a woman Alvar saint?
questions.explanation: Andal's songs are widely sung and express her love for Vishnu.

questions.text: What does the term 'Dargah' mean?
questions.explanation: Dargahs became places of pilgrimage for people of all religions.

questions.text: The Great Sufi saints of Central Asia included:
questions.explanation: These thinkers greatly influenced Sufi thought in India.

questions.text: Varkari sect is associated with the pilgrimage to:
questions.explanation: It is an biannual pilgrimage to the Vitthala temple in Maharashtra.

questions.text: Hagiography refers to:
questions.explanation: Hagiographies of Bhakti saints are used as sources by historians today.

questions.text: The language used by Kabir in his verses was:
questions.explanation: This made his messages accessible to the ordinary people.

questions.text: Which Guru compiled the 'Adi Granth'?
questions.explanation: Guru Arjan compiled the verses of the first four Gurus and other saints in 1604.

questions.text: The term 'Dharmsal' used by Guru Nanak is now known as:
questions.explanation: Dharmsal was the place of worship and assembly established by Nanak.

questions.text: The major emphasis of the Bhakti and Sufi movements was on:
options: Personal devotion and love for God
questions.explanation: Both movements emphasized a direct, emotional bond between the devotee and the Divine.
